100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 30, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as unrest continued across the nation due to a general strike that had paralyzed key industries for weeks. In the United States, the Supreme Court ruled that states could regulate the hours of women workers, a significant victory for labor rights advocates. Meanwhile, in Germany, the political climate remained tense as the Weimar Republic struggled with economic instability and rising extremist movements.
